2605.29473.title: Inform, Coach, Relate, Listen: Auditing LLM Caregiving Support Roles
2605.29473.paper_summary: Language models are increasingly being deployed for conversational support in informal caregiving contexts, where interactions often extend beyond information-seeking: caregivers seek emotional reassurance, guidance, and help, while navigating uncertain, relationally complex care decisions. Yet most safety evaluations assess model behavior under generic prompts, leaving a critical question unexamined: does a model's safety profile change with its support role? We study this by operationalizing four expert-reviewed support roles grounded in social support theory: Inform, Coach, Relate, and Listen, and comparing them against two baseline controls: a basic prompting condition and a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) condition. We evaluate across three language models (GPT-4o-mini, Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct, and MedGemma-1.5-4b-it) on 5,000 real-world queries from online Alzheimer's Disease and Related Dementias (ADRD) communities. We find that the LLM's support role systematically shapes both the prevalence and composition of interactional risks. Furthermore, a human evaluation study reveals a perceived quality--safety tension: more directive, information-oriented roles are rated as more helpful and trustworthy despite exhibiting elevated interactional risk profiles. We release ~90,000 support role-conditioned model responses with risk annotations as an ecologically grounded resource for research on safer LLM-mediated conversational support.
2605.29473.paper_summary_zh: 語言模型越來越多地被用於非正式照護環境中的對話支持，在這些情境中，互動通常超越了尋求資訊的範疇：照護者尋求情感上的安慰、指導和幫助，同時在不確定且關係複雜的照護決策中進行導航。<lb/>然而，大多數安全評估是在一般提示下評估模型行為，這留下一個關鍵問題未被檢視：模型的安全特徵是否會因其支持角色而改變？<lb/>我們通過將社會支持理論中的四個專家審核的支持角色具體化來研究這一問題：告知、教練、關聯和傾聽，並將它們與兩個基準控制進行比較：基本提示條件和檢索增強生成（RAG）條件。<lb/>我們在三個語言模型（GPT-4o-mini、Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct 和 MedGemma-1.5-4b-it）上評估來自在線阿茲海默症及相關癡呆症（ADRD）社區的 5,000 個真實查詢。<lb/>我們發現，LLM 的支持角色系統性地影響了互動風險的普遍性和組成。此外，一項人類評估研究揭示了一種感知的質量—安全張力：更具指導性、以資訊為導向的角色儘管表現出較高的互動風險特徵，但仍被評為更有幫助和更值得信賴。<lb/>我們釋出約 90,000 個支持角色條件下的模型回應，並附有風險註解，作為一個生態基礎的資源，用於研究更安全的 LLM 媒介對話支持。
2605.29473.author: Drishti Goel et.al.
2605.29473.authors: Drishti Goel, Agam Goyal, Veda Duddu, Olivia Pal, Jeongah Lee, Qiuyue Joy Zhong, Violeta J. Rodriguez, Daniel S. Brown, Dong Whi Yoo, Ravi Karkar, Koustuv Saha
